violation_condition: Two participants at a resolved barrier producing different results for the same non-monotonic query.
assumptions: All participants have exchanged all datoms up to the cut point before resolution is declared.
The barrier protocol guarantees that every in-flight message is delivered before resolution.

violation_condition: A barrier that neither resolves nor times out — i.e., hangs indefinitely.
assumptions: The timeout parameter is finite and strictly positive.
The runtime clock is monotonic and cannot stall.

violation_condition: Two different topologies (star, ring, mesh) producing different consistent cuts for the same participants and datom sets.
assumptions: All topologies use the same barrier primitives: initiate, report frontier, exchange deltas, resolve.
The datom set is finite and identical at barrier initiation across all topology comparisons.

violation_condition: A barrier operation that completes (resolved or timed-out) without creating a Barrier entity in the store.
assumptions: The store is accessible to the barrier coordinator for the full duration of the barrier protocol.
The Barrier entity schema is pre-defined in the store.

violation_condition: A Barriered query executing against a timed-out or nonexistent barrier, or against an unresolved barrier.
assumptions: The query engine can distinguish resolved from timed-out barriers by inspecting the Barrier entity.
QueryMode::Barriered carries the barrier_id as a typed reference, not a raw string.
coordination_free
stratum_classified
at_least_once_delivery
sync_barrier_blocking

violation_condition: A signal is emitted but no corresponding datom with entity type :signal/* and matching type/source/target/severity attributes exists in the store.
assumptions: The emit_signal function is the only codepath through which signals are created.
The store is available (not in a crash state) when a signal is emitted.

violation_condition: A Confusion signal is emitted and the agent proceeds to the next action without executing the associate → query → assemble pipeline within the same agent cycle.
assumptions: The agent cycle is a single-threaded execution loop where signal interception is synchronous.
Re-association is strictly bounded in time and cannot itself cause a Confusion signal (no infinite loop).

violation_condition: A subscription pattern matches a signal, but the callback is never invoked (not even in the next refresh cycle).
assumptions: The subscription has not been explicitly removed before the signal arrived.
Debounced subscriptions: 'never invoked' means even after the debounce window has elapsed.

violation_condition: A Critical-severity conflict signal is silently resolved by an automated mechanism (lattice-join or LWW) without human or agent review.
assumptions: The severity field is set at signal emission time and is not mutable after recording.
The routing tier mapping is configured as datoms and thus auditable.

violation_condition: Two incomparable lattice values (e.g., confirmed and refuted in a challenge-verdict diamond) merge to produce the lattice-top element, but no signal of the corresponding type (Conflict or DeliberationTurn) is emitted within the same transaction.
assumptions: Diamond lattice join implementations carry signal-emission as a side effect — this is a structural commitment about the lattice join code, not a post-hoc check.
The set of diamond lattices is fixed: challenge-verdict, finding-lifecycle, proposal-lifecycle.

violation_condition: A divergence is detected by some mechanism but no signal is emitted, leaving that divergence class invisible to the resolution layer.
assumptions: All eight divergence types in the reconciliation taxonomy are detectable by the system.
Temporal and Procedural divergences are surfaced via UncertaintySpike and GoalDrift respectively — the indirection is acceptable coverage.
schema_as_data

violation_condition: A bilateral cycle produces a lower fitness score F(S_{n+1}) < F(S_n) than the previous cycle.
assumptions: Documenting a gap as a residual (with rationale) counts as a non-decreasing fitness operation — i.e., residual documentation is defined to not decrease F(S).
The fitness function F(S) is deterministic given the same store state.

violation_condition: A bilateral cycle evaluates fewer than five coherence conditions (C1–C5), leaving a coherence dimension unchecked.
assumptions: All five conditions (spec self-consistency, impl satisfies spec, spec matches intent, agent agreement, process adherence) are checkable by automated query.
The Intent→Spec boundary (C3) is checkable — but see ADR-BILATERAL-003 which admits this requires human judgment, a potential tension.

violation_condition: The system supports branching for competing implementations but requires strictly linear (non-branching) spec modifications, or vice versa.
assumptions: Forward branching (spec → competing implementations) and backward branching (impl → competing spec updates) are both supported by the MERGE namespace branching mechanism.
The Datalog query apparatus is shared and not direction-specific.

violation_condition: A gap appears in the divergence map for two consecutive bilateral cycles without being either resolved or documented as a residual with uncertainty marker and rationale.
assumptions: A 'cycle' is precisely the CYCLE transition (FORWARD_SCAN + BACKWARD_SCAN + COMPUTE_FITNESS).
Residual documentation requires both an uncertainty marker and a rationale — not just one.

violation_condition: A test runs but its result (pass/fail, error, frontier) is not transacted into the store as a datom.
assumptions: Tests are executed within an environment that has store access.
The store transact is not skipped on test failure — even failure results must be recorded.

provisional_claim: Fitness function weights V=0.18, C=0.18, D=0.18, H=0.13, K=0.13, I=0.08, U=0.12 are the correct empirical calibration.
impact_if_wrong: F(S) could misrepresent true spec quality, leading bilateral cycles to declare convergence when meaningful divergence remains. The monotonic-convergence invariant (INV-BILATERAL-001) might be satisfied numerically while the system remains incoherent in practice.

provisional_claim: Default equal weights across the four boundary gaps in D(spec, impl) = Σᵢ wᵢ × |boundary_gap(i)| are appropriate.
impact_if_wrong: Total divergence metric could over- or under-weight specific boundary types, making remediation targets misleading. Projects with high axiological drift but equal weights might appear healthier than they are.
